label: Megastore Clean
description: Catalog-heavy ecommerce variant with cleaner navigation and retail clarity.
signatureMotif: clean retail IA, strong faceting rhythm, and conversion-first clarity
promptHints: Optimize for product discovery, filtering, and cart clarity before decorative storytelling.
Use a retailer mindset: navigation, categories, and trust must read instantly.

layouts: Use a utility-first homepage with a compact mega-nav, category tiles row, promotional strip, and dense product grid above the fold.
Build PLP pages as a 25/75 split with a persistent left filter rail, top sort bar, and four-column product cards.
Compose PDPs with a two-column gallery-and-purchase block, sticky buy box on the right, and trust details stacked beneath CTA.
Structure cart and checkout as a distraction-reduced flow with item summary left, order totals right, and clear progress steps above.
Use category landing pages with a featured banner, subcategory chips, brand logos row, and product grid starting immediately after intro.
motifs: Use bright white card surfaces, soft gray section fills, and thin border strokes to keep the catalog feeling organized and scannable.
Show dense faceting UI with pill filters, compact dropdowns, and tidy sort controls that read like retail tools, not editorial decor.
Make product cards uniform with medium radius corners, image-first hierarchy, restrained shadows, and price-actions aligned in predictable rows.
Keep navigation visually explicit with stacked utility bars, category labels, badges, and count indicators that surface retail hierarchy instantly.
antiPatterns: Avoid oversized editorial heroes or story-first landing sections that push categories and product discovery below the initial viewport.
Never use glassmorphism, blurred overlays, or atmospheric gradients that reduce price clarity and make filters feel secondary.
Avoid sparse boutique layouts with huge whitespace and tiny assortments; this variant should feel broad, stocked, and easy to browse.
Do not hide filtering inside a single modal-first experience when desktop layouts have room for a persistent faceting rail.
